privileged_info_summary: The task uses 2020 US household income distribution data from official Census sources (Statista/CPS data). The income shares are nearly accurate with minor discrepancies: lowest 3.0% (correct), second 8.1% (official: 8.2%), third 14.0% (correct), fourth 22.6% (correct), highest 52.3% (official: 52.2%). The Lorenz curve points are internally consistent with the task's stated income distribution. The 2020 US Gini coefficient was officially 0.488 according to the Census Bureau, which falls within the task's expected range of 0.43 to 0.49 (at the upper boundary). The income data is real, sourced from the US Census Bureau Current Population Survey (CPS), not synthetic.
